approach: Rename the layout capability to template across the live surface only; backend native vocabulary (herdr pane layout, tmux window_layout) and history (plans, research, ledgers) are explicitly out of scope.
Lead with the spec prose: the node's own description must name arrangement, environment and launch commands, since the undersold name is the whole argument for the rename.
Treat the wholesale .feature rename as a rewrite edit class, not additive: it rewrites frozen scenario text, so it requires a ratified freeze re-open before any content edit.
Verify the rename against a captured baseline of 102 scenarios / 7 outlines / 18 stages rather than trusting a green structural verdict.
Clean break on the CLI verb: no deprecated layout alias, justified by the package being unpublished at 0.0.0.

why.blast: large — the whole layout capability surface: 6 src modules plus tests, cli.ts command group and flag, the spec node and its 102-scenario frozen suite, and two website pages. CLI verbs change name, which is user-visible.
why.novelty: low-medium — mechanically a rename, but it carries two non-mechanical judgments: the backend-native-vocabulary boundary the brief did not name, and the edit class of a wholesale frozen-suite rename.
why.floor: leash set to auto-none because the mission opens on a freeze re-open of an implemented node's frozen suite — a status write the conductor does not own. Ratification is available live in-session.

why.reopen: Owner ratified the freeze re-open in-session: a wholesale vocabulary rename of template.feature (formerly layout.feature) and mux.feature is a rewrite edit class, not additive — rename-only, meaning preserved, no scenario narrowed.
why.verification: Did NOT trust the tool verdict, per brief. gherkin-cli diff and classify-edit-class BOTH falsely reported the renamed suite as addOnly/ADDITIVE (102 added / 0 removed) because the file moved paths and the classifier does not consult git's rename pairing. Verified by hand instead: template.feature preserves 102 scenarios / 7 outlines / 18 stages; mux.feature preserves 146 scenarios / 46 outlines / 19 stages. Proved rename-equivalence for both by re-deriving the sed substitution from the pre-rename baseline and diffing to zero (every changed line a one-for-one word swap; git similarity 88%, balanced 82 ins / 82 del on template.feature).
why.boundary: Backend-native vocabulary preserved on the native side of the boundary the brief did not name: herdr `pane layout` verb, `layout.panes[].rect`, `layout.splits[]`, `result.layout.panes` JSON, tmux `#{window_layout}` all kept in session.herdr.ts/session.tmux.ts and their test fixtures. Only our-concept references renamed.
why.floor: no floor fires: no scenario weakened or deleted (no Clearance); package unpublished at 0.0.0 so Compatibility does not fire (per root spec's own recorded stance); no logical contradiction introduced (no Conflict).

summary: classify-edit-class reports a renamed frozen .feature as ADDITIVE (self-clears) instead of consulting git's rename pairing; a renamed frozen suite passes the spec gate's freeze guard unexamined. Hole in the freeze guard, surfaced by this mission's wholesale rename.
